figures.caption: Figure 1. Runtime–memory scaling in streaming 3D reconstruction. Bars show per-frame runtime (ms) and lines show KV cache memory (GB, log scale) as the stream grows. Compared with Causal-VGGT, STAC reduces KV cache growth and stabilizes per-frame runtime as the stream length increases.
Figure 4. Qualitative results on streaming inputs from the 7-scenes and NRGBD datasets.

Table 1. Quantitative results for point cloud reconstruction on the NRGBD and 7 Scenes datasets. Integrating STAC into STream3R and StreamVGGT substantially improves memory and runtime efficiency while preserving reconstruction quality. W8 denotes a sliding window size of 8. W22 and W26 indicate larger windows for fair total memory, though our method uses less memory at runtime. Mem: main values report total memory footprint for comparison; for STAC, this includes the spatial token cache storage, while parentheses show its runtime usage. FPS: end-to-end throughput measured over the full reconstruction pipeline, from image input to the final 3D outputs.

Figure 2. Spatio-temporal attention sparsity. Representative attention patterns in the global causal attention map of CausalVGGT, retaining the top 1024 keys per query for visualization. (a) Spatial attention aligned with camera motion; (b) Persistent focus on first-frame tokens as global references; (c) Temporal anchoring via tokens from semantically stable landmark frames; (d) Longrange attention to camera tokens encoding global context.
Table 2. Quantitative camera pose estimation results on the Sintel, TUM Dynamics, and ScanNet datasets.

Figure 3. Overview of STAC. Our framework reconstructs 3D scenes online using spatio-temporal token caching and chunk-based causal inference. (a) The Causal-VGGT module processes ViT-tokenized frames in each chunk using causal attention over the working temporal cache Mtemp and spatial cache Mspat retrieved from a 3D voxel grid. (b) During inference, Working Temporal Token Caching updates token scores after each KV cache access, retaining high-scoring anchor tokens Manchor while preserving first-frame reference tokens Mrefer and sliding-window tokens Mwindow, and evicting the rest. (c) Long-term Spatial Token Caching routes evicted tokens with 3D coordinates from the Head Decoder to many-to-one aggregation in E or one-to-one merging into G. When G is full, re-merging frees a slot for the incoming merged token, and the updated representations are stored in a 3D voxel grid for future retrieval.
